skill.name: AI Image Generation
skill.description: Generate and edit images on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes requests across 11+ AI models including FLUX 2, GPT Image 2, Nano Banana, Seedream, Qwen Image, and Wan 2-7. Supports text-to-image and image-to-image workflows with model-specific prompting patterns.
skill.summary: Smart router for AI image generation across 11+ models via the runcomfy CLI.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a markdown documentation file that routes AI image generation requests to the runcomfy CLI. The 309 static findings are almost entirely false positives: the 'shell backtick execution' detections (213) are markdown code-block formatting, the 'hardcoded URL' detections (63) are documentation links to runcomfy.com model pages, and the 'path traversal' / 'hidden file' detections reference the documented token storage path. The skill declares a restrictive allowed-tools scope (Bash(runcomfy *)) and includes an explicit Security & Privacy section documenting its threat model. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious code patterns were found.

low_findings.title: External command execution via runcomfy CLI
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run the runcomfy CLI, which makes network requests to the RunComfy Model API and writes output files. This is the skill's intended behavior, not a vulnerability. The allowed-tools scope is restricted to Bash(runcomfy *).

low_findings.title: Outbound network calls to RunComfy API
low_findings.description: The CLI connects to model-api.runcomfy.net and related domains. The skill documents its outbound endpoints (allowlist) explicitly in the Security & Privacy section. No telemetry or unsolicited callbacks.

low_findings.title: Filesystem output to user-specified directory
low_findings.description: Generated images are written to a user-provided --output-dir. The skill instructs the user to choose the directory; no hidden or system paths are accessed. CLI enforces a 2 GiB per-file download cap.

content.value_statement: Picking the right AI image model for a task is hard, and the CLI syntax for each is different. This skill routes your request to the best model and ships the exact invoke command for text-to-image and image-to-image generation.
actual_capabilities: Routes text-to-image requests to the best-fit model from 11+ options including FLUX 2 Klein, GPT Image 2, Nano Banana 2, and Seedream 5
Routes image-to-image and edit requests to Nano Banana Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, Seedream Edit, or FLUX Kontext Pro
Generates the correct runcomfy run CLI invocation with model-specific JSON input schema
Applies documented prompting patterns per model (typography, photoreal, multilingual, multi-reference)
Supports aspect ratio, resolution, and output format configuration for each model's schema
Covers common workflows: brand posters, photoreal portraits, storyboard batches, multilingual creatives
limitations: Does not run on its own; requires the runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated
Does not perform mask-driven inpainting; delegates to the separate image-edit skill
Does not cover video generation; delegates to ai-video-generation and image-to-video skills
Image quality, cost, and latency depend on the chosen model tier and resolution settings

use_cases.title: Marketing designers creating campaign assets
use_cases.description: Generate brand posters, product shots, and multilingual launch creatives by routing to GPT Image 2 for typography or Seedream for photoreal.

use_cases.title: Developers building AI-powered apps
use_cases.description: Integrate image generation into workflows by running the documented runcomfy CLI commands with correct schemas for each model.

use_cases.title: Content creators producing social media visuals
use_cases.description: Produce moodboards, storyboard frames, and concept art at speed using FLUX 2 Klein 4B or Nano Banana 2 with batch settings.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a photoreal portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to generate a photoreal portrait of a woman by a window with soft natural light using Seedream 5 Lite.

prompt_templates.title: Create a brand poster with exact text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to create a 3:2 landscape poster with the headline 'AURORA Spring 2026' in bold white sans-serif on navy using GPT Image 2.

prompt_templates.title: Batch 4 concept images for moodboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to generate 4 concept images of a ceramic mug on marble at 0.5K resolution using Nano Banana 2 with num_images 4.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing image to change background
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-image-generation skill to convert the background of my portrait to a rainy neon cyberpunk street while preserving identity, using Nano Banana 2 Edit.

output_examples.input: Generate a photoreal portrait of a woman by a window with soft natural light
output_examples.output: Routes to Seedream 5 Lite. Runs the CLI with a prompt describing 85mm portrait, natural light, and shallow depth of field. Output: a photoreal PNG written to ./out.

output_examples.input: Make a poster with the exact headline 'SALE 50% OFF' in bold white text
output_examples.output: Routes to GPT Image 2. Runs the CLI with a prompt quoting the headline literally and size 1536_1024 for landscape. Output: a poster image with correctly rendered text.

output_examples.input: Edit this product photo to have a beach background
output_examples.output: Routes to Nano Banana 2 Edit. Runs the CLI with the user-provided image URL and a prompt instructing background replacement while preserving the product. Output: edited image in ./out.

best_practices: Specify the intended use case (typography, photoreal, batch, multilingual) so the skill can pick the right model
For in-image text, always quote the exact text string and name the script (e.g., Japanese kana, Cyrillic)
Use low resolution (0.5K) for ideation rounds and high resolution (2K or 4K) only for final delivery to control cost
anti_patterns: Do not pass arbitrary width and height values to GPT Image 2; it only accepts three fixed sizes
Do not enable web_search by default; only enable it when the prompt references real-world products, places, or people
Do not use the same model for everything; different models excel at typography vs photoreal vs illustration

faq.question: Which model should I use for in-image text?
faq.answer: Use GPT Image 2. It has the best typography rendering for Latin, Japanese kana, Cyrillic, and Arabic scripts.

faq.question: How do I install the runcomfy CLI?
faq.answer: Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli for a global install, or npx -y @runcomfy/cli for zero-install usage. Then run runcomfy login to authenticate.

faq.question: Can I edit an existing image?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the image-to-image routes: Nano Banana 2 Edit for general edits, GPT Image 2 Edit for multilingual text swaps, or FLUX Kontext Pro for single-shot precise local edits.

faq.question: What is the default model?
faq.answer: FLUX 2 Klein 9B for text-to-image and Nano Banana 2 Edit for image-to-image. Both are strong all-rounders for general use.

faq.question: How do I control cost?
faq.answer: Use lower resolution (0.5K) for ideation, fewer steps for step-distilled models, and cheaper tiers like FLUX 2 Klein 4B for batch concepting.

faq.question: Is the runcomfy CLI safe to use?
faq.answer: The CLI stores your API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. You can also set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable to avoid file storage in CI environments.
